scope: 143 previously automatic KOLESTONE records whose productType changed from hair_color_shade to permanent_color
conclusion: Compatible: permanent_color is supported as a shade-bearing, tonal-classification-eligible product type after downstream updates.

downstreamFindings.finding: Database schema stores primary_product_type as TEXT, not an enum. Shared Product Truth TypeScript and normalizer taxonomy now include permanent_color, demi_permanent, acidic_toner, and direct_dye.

Classification output now carries productType separately from shadeBearing and tonalClassificationEligible. All 143 KOLESTONE records have permanent_color + shadeBearing=true + tonalClassificationEligible=true.
All 143 records retain normalized shades, numeric levels, and manufacturerSpecific tonalProfile objects.
Admin Product Database filter and shared product drawers now include detailed color product types. Permanent Color is visible instead of falling through to Other.
build-color-intelligence-dictionary COLOR_PRODUCT_TYPES now includes permanent_color, demi_permanent, acidic_toner, and direct_dye.
No remaining productType === hair_color_shade or equivalent hard equality checks were found in active code after refactor.
Legacy generated POL shade-map artifacts still contain historical hair_color_shade values; they are static historical reports, not Wella M5 production writes.

tests: scripts/lib/m5-classification/__tests__/product-classifier.test.ts: KOLESTONE permanent_color remains shadeBearing and tonalClassificationEligible with tonalProfile.
scripts/lib/product-catalog/__tests__/canonical-product-truth.test.ts: permanent_color identities remain in shade intelligence with shade decoding fields.
